prompt: From the invoice below, extract a JSON object with EXACTLY these keys: vendor_name (string, the party that ISSUED the invoice / seller, NOT the bill-to customer), invoice_number (string), invoice_date (ISO date YYYY-MM-DD), currency (ISO 4217 code, e.g. USD/EUR/GBP), subtotal (number), tax_amount (number, or null if no tax/VAT line is itemized), total_due (number), payment_terms_days (integer number of days, or null if not stated), po_number (string, or null if no purchase-order number is present). Output ONLY the JSON object, no commentary. Copy numbers exactly as printed; do not recompute. Use null wherever the invoice is silent; do NOT substitute a customer reference, account number, or any other identifier for a purchase-order number that is not there.

inputs.name: invoice.txt
inputs.content:
BLUE RIDGE SUPPLY CO.
47 Kiln Road, Sheffield, UK
INVOICE

Bill To: Aperture Labs, 400 Innovation Way, Austin TX, USA
Invoice #: BRS-2024-00917
Invoice Date: 14 March 2024
Customer Ref: CR-55210
Account No: 88123

Description                        Qty   Unit     Amount
Recycled A4 paper, case             12   18.50    222.00
Toner cartridge (black)              4   64.00    256.00
Delivery surcharge                   1   15.00     15.00

Subtotal:            EUR 493.00
VAT (20%):           EUR  98.60
Total Due:           EUR 591.60

Payment terms: Net 30. Please remit within 30 days of the invoice date.
Late payments subject to 1.5% monthly interest.
Questions? billing@blueridge.example

grading.guidance: Gold JSON: {"vendor_name":"Blue Ridge Supply Co.","invoice_number":"BRS-2024-00917","invoice_date":"2024-03-14","currency":"EUR","subtotal":493.00,"tax_amount":98.60,"total_due":591.60,"payment_terms_days":30,"po_number":null}. Two deliberate traps: (1) po_number MUST be null — the invoice shows a 'Customer Ref' (CR-55210) and an 'Account No' (88123) but NO purchase-order number; treating either as po_number is a hallucination. (2) vendor_name is the ISSUER 'Blue Ridge Supply Co.', NOT the bill-to customer 'Aperture Labs'. Also note the currency is EUR (not USD, despite US addresses), the date must be ISO 2024-03-14, and payment_terms_days is 30 (from 'Net 30'). A 10 matches all nine fields with correct types, the exact EUR currency code, tax_amount 98.60, po_number null, and vendor_name = the seller. Heavily penalize po_number set to CR-55210 / 88123 / any non-null value, vendor_name = Aperture Labs, currency USD, a null or recomputed tax_amount, a wrong/missing date, or extra keys and prose outside the JSON.
